verifactu.securityNote: The certificate and its password are stored encrypted (AES-256-GCM) and only used on the server to authenticate the connection to AEAT. They are never exposed to the browser.

tutorial.viewTutorialHint: Walk through how Archivum works step by step.
tutorial.helpSectionDesc: Learn how to get the most out of Archivum.

welcome.title: Welcome to Archivum
welcome.description: Your platform to issue invoices and archive all of your business paperwork. This tutorial walks you through every section step by step.
steps: The sidebar holds every section: Dashboard, Clients, Library, Search, Inventory, Invoicing, Upload and Users.
The Dashboard summarizes this month's revenue, pending payments and recent documents.
You can switch language (ES/EN) and replay this tutorial anytime from Settings.
welcome.tip: Use the ← → arrow keys to move through the tutorial.

companies.title: 1. Create your clients
companies.description: Before invoicing or archiving, register the clients you work with.
steps: Open “Clients” and click “New client”.
Fill in the name and, crucially, the CIF/NIF (tax ID): it is required to issue invoices.
Add address, postal code, city and province so they appear on the invoice.
Use search and the sector filters to find them quickly once you have many.
companies.tip: You can also create a client on the fly while issuing an invoice.

upload.title: 2. Upload and archive documents
upload.description: Store received invoices, delivery notes, orders and receipts in your central archive.
steps: Go to “Upload” and drag the file (PDF or image) onto the drop zone, or click to pick it.
Choose the related client and the document type (invoice, delivery note, order, receipt).
Enter the document number, date and amount.
Set the status (paid, pending, overdue…) and save. It is now archived and searchable.
upload.tip: The “pending” status with a due date triggers automatic alerts.

library.title: 3. Browse your library
library.description: The Library is the full list of every document you have archived.
steps: Open “Library” to see all documents ordered by date.
A colored dot shows each one's status (green paid, amber pending, red overdue).
If you have overdue invoices, a red badge appears in the sidebar.
Click any document to view the detail, edit it or download it.

inventory.title: 4. Manage your inventory
inventory.description: Save your products and services to reuse them when invoicing, without retyping prices.
steps: In “Inventory” click “New product” and set name, price, VAT and optionally a reference and stock. If you leave the reference empty, it is generated automatically.
Assign a category to keep the catalog organized.
Use the filter panel on the left to narrow by category, price, VAT or stock.
When creating an invoice you can pick these products and their data fills in automatically.
inventory.tip: Enable stock tracking to see the available units of each product.

invoicing.title: 5. Issue invoices with VeriFactu
invoicing.description: Create legal invoices compliant with the AEAT VeriFactu system.
steps: First upload your digital certificate in Settings → VeriFactu (just once).
In “Invoicing” click “New invoice” and choose the client (must have a tax ID).
Add lines manually or from your inventory; VAT and withholding (IRPF) are computed for you.
Click “Issue”: the invoice gets its chained fingerprint and AEAT verification QR.
invoicing.tip: Use search and filters to find any issued invoice instantly.

search.title: 6. Find anything
search.description: The Advanced Search locates any document with precise filters.
steps: Type in the bar by number, company, tax ID, amount or notes.
Combine filters by type, status, date range and amount range.
Sort the results by date or amount.
Export the result to CSV or Excel for your accounting.

team.title: 7. Work as a team
team.description: Invite your team and control who sees and does what.
steps: In “Users” (admins only) invite a member by email.
Assign a role: Admin (full access), Manager or Viewer (read and download only).
The admin decides which folders each manager can access.
Manage your plan's extra users from Settings → Billing.
team.tip: That's it! You now know Archivum. Start by creating your first client.

empresasCreateFirst.title: Create your first client
empresasCreateFirst.description: Group documents by client or department. The free plan includes 1 client.

usersInviteFirst.title: Invite your team
usersInviteFirst.description: Add admins, members or viewers. Each role has different permissions over clients and folders.

bibliotecaUploadFirst.title: Upload your first document
bibliotecaUploadFirst.description: Your documents will appear here. Start by uploading your first invoice or contract.

subirDropzone.title: Start here
subirDropzone.description: Drag an invoice, contract or receipt here, or click to choose files. AI will extract the data automatically.

subirCompany.title: Link to a client (required)
subirCompany.description: Associate this document with one of your clients. If you haven't created any yet, do it from the Clients section.

subirType.title: Document type (required)
subirType.description: Choose whether it's an invoice, receipt, delivery note, contract, or other. This helps the AI extract data better.

subirDate.title: Issue date
subirDate.description: The date the document was issued. AI may suggest it automatically when you upload the file.

subirAmount.title: Total amount
subirAmount.description: Tax-inclusive amount. You can leave it empty and fill it in later.

subirStatus.title: Document status
subirStatus.description: Pending, paid, overdue… Helps you track each invoice's status.

subirSubmit.title: Archive document
subirSubmit.description: When you're done, click this button to save. The document will appear in your Library.
